phases.title: Phase 1: Foundations
phases.description: Master the command line, version control, and core networking.

days.title: Linux Commands & Scripting
days.explanation: Linux CLI navigation and scripting forms the bedrock of DevOps. Knowing how to move around, write basic automations, and manage permissions saves hours of manual configuration.

commands.desc: List all files including hidden files with details
Grant execution permission to a script
Search logs case-insensitively for errors
Compress log directory into a gzipped tarball

days.setup_script:
mkdir -p /tmp/practice && cd /tmp/practice
echo 'Creating dummy logs...'
echo 'ERROR: Database connection failed' > app.log
echo 'INFO: User logged in' >> app.log
echo 'SUCCESS: Query completed' >> app.log
echo "echo 'Hello World automated!'" > script.sh
chmod +x script.sh
clear
echo 'Linux playground environment is ready!'
tasks: Write a script to automate log compression
Configure user groups and permissions

days.title: Git & Collaborative Workflows
days.explanation: Version control is the heart of collaborative development and deployment. Understanding branching strategies, merge conflicts, and hooks ensures clean code integration.

commands.desc: Create and switch to a new branch
Merge main branch into the current active branch
Rebase current branch on top of main
Show changes between two branches

days.setup_script:
git init my-project && cd my-project
git config --global user.email 'you@example.com'
git config --global user.name 'Your Name'
echo 'Initial Content' > readme.md
git add readme.md && git commit -m 'initial commit'
git checkout -b feature-test
echo 'Feature Content' >> readme.md
git add readme.md && git commit -m 'add feature content'
git checkout main
echo 'Main Content' >> readme.md
git add readme.md && git commit -m 'add conflicting main content'
clear
echo 'Git playground ready! Branch conflict prepared on main branch.'
tasks: Create a repo, create conflict, merge and resolve it
Setup a simple pre-commit hook

days.title: DevOps Networking Essentials
days.explanation: Understanding DNS, HTTP, and load balancing helps debug connectivity issues between microservices, configure secure gateways, and manage routing.

commands.desc: Query DNS record for domain
Inspect HTTP response headers
List all listening TCP and UDP ports
Resolve domain name to IP address

days.setup_script:
echo 'Starting a lightweight Python HTTP server on port 8000...'
python3 -m http.server 8000 &
sleep 2
curl http://localhost:8000
echo 'Use curl or netstat to examine active connections!'
tasks: Dig DNS records for a domain
Run a local python server and map traffic

phases.title: Phase 2: Containerization & Automation
phases.description: Learn to package applications and automate server setup.

days.title: Docker Core Concepts
days.explanation: Containers isolate applications and their environments, making execution consistent from developer laptops to production servers.

commands.desc: Build Docker image from Dockerfile
Run Nginx container in background
Open interactive shell inside container
Create a persistent Docker volume

days.setup_script:
echo 'Setting up Docker challenge directory...' mkdir -p docker-app && cd docker-app cat << 'EOF' > Dockerfile FROM nginx:alpine RUN echo "

Docker Practice

" > /usr/share/nginx/html/index.html EOF clear echo 'Dockerfile environment setup complete.'
tasks: Write a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Node/Python app
Mount database volume locally

days.title: Docker Compose
days.explanation: Multi-container applications require orchestrating databases, backends, and frontends. Docker Compose lets you define and run them with a single YAML config.

commands.desc: Start all services in Compose file in background
Stop containers and remove associated volumes
Follow real-time logs of all services
List status of all compose-managed containers

days.setup_script:
mkdir -p compose-project && cd compose-project
cat << 'EOF' > docker-compose.yml
version: '3'
services:
  web:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "8081:80"
  db:
    image: redis:alpine
EOF
clear
echo 'docker-compose.yml file created! run docker-compose up -d to launch.'
tasks: Spin up a Wordpress + MySQL stack using Compose
Define healthchecks in Compose file

days.title: CI/CD & Jenkins/GitHub Actions
days.explanation: Automation of builds, tests, and deployments reduces human error and shortens the release loop, enabling rapid feedback on code changes.

commands.desc: Trigger pipeline via commit
List recent workflow runs from GitHub CLI
Watch status of active pipeline run

days.setup_script:
mkdir -p .github/workflows && cd .github/workflows
cat << 'EOF' > main.yml
name: CI
on: [push]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v2
      - name: Test
        run: echo "Testing application..."
EOF
clear
echo 'Sample GitHub Actions workflow prepared!'
tasks: Setup GitHub Action to run linter on git push
Build and push docker image to DockerHub automatically

phases.title: Phase 3: Orchestration & Infrastructure
phases.description: Learn production grade systems scaling and IaC.

days.title: Kubernetes Architecture & Pods
days.explanation: Kubernetes scales container management. Pods are the smallest deployable units in K8s, containing one or more tightly-coupled containers sharing resources.

commands.desc: List all pods across all namespaces
Show detailed status of a specific pod
Create or update resources from a YAML file
Retrieve logs for a running container in a pod

days.setup_script:
cat << 'EOF' > pod.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: my-nginx
spec:
  containers:
  - name: nginx
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
EOF
clear
echo 'pod.yaml created. Run: kubectl apply -f pod.yaml to test.'
tasks: Install Minikube or Kind
Deploy an Nginx Pod via YAML configuration

days.title: K8s Deployments & Services
days.explanation: Deployments manage replica counts and rolling updates, while Services provide stable IP addresses and load balancing to route traffic to dynamic Pods.

commands.desc: List deployments in the active namespace
Scale a deployment to 3 replica pods
Expose web deployment via NodePort
Check rolling update progress

days.setup_script:
cat << 'EOF' > deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-web
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: web
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: web
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: nginx
        image: nginx:alpine
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
EOF
clear
echo 'deployment.yaml generated! run kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml'
tasks: Create a Deployment with 3 replicas
Expose deployment using ClusterIP and port-forward to test

days.title: Infrastructure as Code with Terraform
days.explanation: Writing infrastructure as code enables auditing, versioning, and repeatable environments. Terraform tracks state to make changes incremental and safe.

commands.desc: Initialize backend and download required providers
Show execution plan listing proposed resource changes
Apply config changes automatically without confirmation prompt
Tear down and delete all resources managed by Terraform

tasks: Deploy a local Docker container using Terraform
Configure state locking or local file backend

phases.title: Phase 4: Interview & Production Readiness
phases.description: Refine architectures and practice common DevOps interview topics.

days.title: DevOps Interview Q&A & System Design
days.explanation: Redundant architectures, horizontal scaling, and backup policies form the basis of DevOps system design. Practice articulating these trade-offs clearly.

commands.desc: Show how long the system has been running and load averages
Display disk space usage in human-readable format
Check memory statistics and swap utilization
View kernel and system daemon logs with explanations

tasks: Architect a resilient three-tier web application
Review 15 common interview scenarios
